title: Test coverage for internal/irdrift (CI-bound DSL→IR drift guard)

evidence.ref: Audit identified internal/irdrift as CI-bound (every PR via contracts.yml) but untested. Check() shells out to SBCL so the integration test uses exec.LookPath to skip gracefully when SBCL is missing; pure-Go helpers (isGeneratedIR + copyDir + copyFile) are always tested
040-conceptmap-tests - same priority pattern (CI-bound untested package → tests next); D041 finishes the CI-bound untested-package backlog

guards.ref: internal/irdrift/irdrift_test.go - TestIsGeneratedIR (9 positive+negative cases incl. .ir.json suffix + ir/domain/ dir + nested path rejection) + TestCopyDirRoundTrip (4-file nested tree) + TestCopyDirCreatesEmptyDirectories + TestCopyFileWritesBytesExactly (binary-safe round-trip) + TestCopyFileCreatesIntermediateDirectories + TestCopyDirSrcMissingReturnsError + TestCheckIntegrationWithSBCL (end-to-end real-tree, skipped if sbcl not in PATH; runs in CI which installs sbcl)

examples: isGeneratedIR rejects schema/ir.schema.json (wrong dir), domain/x.json (wrong ext), and nested domain/sub/x.ir.json (only direct domain/ children count).
copyDir round-trips a 4-file tree (top.txt + a/sibling.txt + a/b/leaf.txt + a/b/c/d/deep.txt) preserving byte content.
TestCheckIntegrationWithSBCL passes locally and in contracts.yml (which installs SBCL): regenerates the contracts repo's IR into a temp dir and asserts zero drift against committed files.
counterexamples: 3 untested internal/ packages remain (devpattern, jsonutil, knowledge) - all operator-internal (not CI-bound). D041 closes the CI-bound test-coverage backlog; the remaining packages can be addressed in future decisions when their reliability matters.
Tests verify Check's helpers but don't simulate emitter failure paths (e.g. malformed DSL → SBCL error) — covered implicitly by the integration test running against real DSL.
